description:
A Sustainability Specialist at a large corporate campus is evaluating on-site renewable generation options. While solar PV is their primary focus, they are cross-evaluating a mid-sized wind turbine for winter energy generation. The turbine manufacturer offers the selected model with two tower height options: 80 meters and 100 meters. The taller tower exposes the rotor to higher wind speeds due to atmospheric wind shear, but it increases the capital cost of the project.

Calculate the difference in Annual Energy Production (AEP) between the 80m and 100m hub heights using a PySAM script and a bundled NREL .srw wind resource file from the SAM installation directory. Save the script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/hub_height_analysis.py and export the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/hub_height_results.json. The JSON file must include the keys "aep_80m_kwh", "aep_100m_kwh", "percent_increase" (calculated as ((100m - 80m) / 80m) * 100), and "resource_file_used" (the absolute path to the .srw file).
